programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Go hunting with flyers who excel at making mid-air catches. Watch the metamorphosis of the acrobatic emperor dragonfly, admire the barn owl’s stealthy approach to prey, and catch a wandering salamander’s death-defying leap into the abyss.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Check in with animals who are winners at the aerial mating game. Take flight with a bee crafting a custom love potion, watch a draco lizard soar to fend off rivals, and witness the graceful pas de deux that bonds a pair of Japanese cranes for life.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Plot a course for adventure as animals go on remarkable journeys through the skies. Navigate a wooden labyrinth with a speedy goshawk, join a sandgrouse’s trek to a distant watering hole, and see a murmuration of starlings mesmerize their nemesis.

credits.director: Sally Cryer, Adam Simon, Dani Godwin: Producer/Director, Keeley Hawes: Narrator

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Meet a flock of cedar waxwings who have taken over the center’s aviary and are keeping the staff busy with endless demands for food. Then the rescue team works with the Ministry of Natural Resources to relocate a coyote that’s too people-friendly.

credits.director: Stacey Hatch, David Hatch, Sharone Ostrovsky:Producers

programme.title: Australia's Hidden Islands
programme.desc: Just to the south of the Great Barrier Reef and the Tropic of Capricorn, Fraser Island lies like a sprawling lizard. Some 120 kilometers long and up to 25 kilometers wide, Fraser Island is now the largest sand island on Earth. Somehow on this nutrient-poor bed of sand life has thrived. Dingoes patrol its forests in search of wallabies while white-bellied sea eagles scour the beaches from above. From the lakes to the forests and its crystal clear streams - Fraser Island is a unique mosaic of unlikely habitats that have earned it World Heritage Status.

credits.director: Michael Tear, Bettina Dalton:Producers

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The ocean sprawls across 70 percent of our planet and is home to as many as one million known species of plants and animals. However, it is the smallest of ocean creatures that underpins the world’s largest food web – plankton. These microscopic organisms drift through the ocean’s diverse habitats, triggering feeding cycles in their wake. Without them, life in the ocean would cease to exist.

credits.director: Dan Hughes:Director

programme.title: Land of Gremlins
programme.desc: This is the story of a day in the life of an Indris Family following a mother, father, and their three offspring around their home in the rainforest of Mantadia National Park in Madagascar.

credits.director: Ellen Windermuth:Producer

programme.title: Land of Gremlins
programme.desc: This film travels around the island visiting some of its most striking landscapes to explore how Madagascar’s species have evolved over time. Madagascar is a strangely beautiful and unique world with a staggering array of habitats and special landscapes.

credits.director: Ellen Windermuth:Producer

programme.title: Land of Gremlins
programme.desc: Explore the lives of Madagascar’s cold-blooded creatures. Chameleons, frog, lizards, and snakes have all adapted in order to survive on the island. We take a look at how they find food, reproduce, and avoid predators.

credits.director: Ellen Windermuth:Producer

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: In some of the most-remote regions on the planet, researchers dive deep and brave danger to unlock secret shark behaviours, challenge myths about sharks, and fight for the protection of these supreme predators – before it’s too late.

credits.director: Dan Hughes:Director

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Often portrayed as mindless eating machines prone to attacking humans, sharks are more complex and intelligent than previously thought. Find out how these graceful top predators interact, choose friends, and even teach other sharks.

credits.director: Dan Hughes:Director

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: From manicured rice fields to wild tropical rainforests, the Mekong River is the source of life for eastern Thailand. Watch gibbons negotiate family dynamics, and see how the pit viper creates danger for the region’s delicate living networks.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: Bisected by the Mekong’s mighty waters, Cambodia is defined by its relationship to this great river. See why the country is a biodiversity hot spot and explore the secret refuges of animal populations that survive in its untouched natural habitats.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong is Cambodia’s lifeline, especially during the scorching dry season, when precious moisture is hard to come by. Learn how all manner of flying creatures, from birds to bats, manage resources until the rainy season brings sweet relief.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The final country on the Mekong’s journey is the home of the rivers mighty delta. A large deposit of rich nutrients carried downstream from hundreds of kilometres makes this region, the jewel of Vietnam’s crown, a biological treasure trove.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: The Mekong Delta is one of the largest on Earth. Formed over the last 8000 years, it fans out and discharges into the South China Sea via nine channels. These channels split and shape the coastline, creating unique habitats for a variety of animals.

credits.director: Leila Monks:Producer

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Learn how South America’s rich diversity of weather fuels an even richer diversity of life. Examine the Amazon rainforest’s oxygen-breathing trees, follow the arc of a vast cloud river, and meet flamingos that thrive in the sun’s scorching UV rays.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E1 -January-February
programme.desc: In the early months of the year, the Earth’s axial tilt brings the height of summer in some places and the depths of winter in others. See how life struggles to survive under these extreme conditions.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E2 -March-April
programme.desc: Travel around the globe to witness the atmospheric shifts that take place in March, from the wave of cherry blossoms that unfold across Japan to the final raft of green sea turtles that arrive on the Great Barrier Reef islands to lay eggs.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E3 -May-June
programme.desc: Watch summer unfold in the Northern Hemisphere, while regions close to the equator enter the wet season and Southern Africa goes dry, creating scarcity for some creatures and bounty for others.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E4 -July-August
programme.desc: Witness some of the planet’s most dramatic natural shifts in July, from the Arctic ice sheets that come crashing down during the summer melt to the feeding frenzy when sockeye salmon reach the end of their journey in Alaska.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E5 -September-October
programme.desc: Travel around the world to see another seasonal change unfold. The Northern Hemisphere moves into autumn, while Australia witnesses a rebirth of life, and mating humpbacks fill the warming tropical seas of Tonga.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E6 -November-December
programme.desc: Witness some of the most incredible natural events in November, from the millions of Monarch butterflies that converge in Mexico’s forests to the deep fog that shrouds the city of Dubai, signalling the start of winter.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Learn how South America’s rich diversity of weather fuels an even richer diversity of life. Examine the Amazon rainforest’s oxygen-breathing trees, follow the arc of a vast cloud river, and meet flamingos that thrive in the sun’s scorching UV rays.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E1 -January-February
programme.desc: In the early months of the year, the Earth’s axial tilt brings the height of summer in some places and the depths of winter in others. See how life struggles to survive under these extreme conditions.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E2 -March-April
programme.desc: Travel around the globe to witness the atmospheric shifts that take place in March, from the wave of cherry blossoms that unfold across Japan to the final raft of green sea turtles that arrive on the Great Barrier Reef islands to lay eggs.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E3 -May-June
programme.desc: Watch summer unfold in the Northern Hemisphere, while regions close to the equator enter the wet season and Southern Africa goes dry, creating scarcity for some creatures and bounty for others.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E4 -July-August
programme.desc: Witness some of the planet’s most dramatic natural shifts in July, from the Arctic ice sheets that come crashing down during the summer melt to the feeding frenzy when sockeye salmon reach the end of their journey in Alaska.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E5 -September-October
programme.desc: Travel around the world to see another seasonal change unfold. The Northern Hemisphere moves into autumn, while Australia witnesses a rebirth of life, and mating humpbacks fill the warming tropical seas of Tonga.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E6 -November-December
programme.desc: Witness some of the most incredible natural events in November, from the millions of Monarch butterflies that converge in Mexico’s forests to the deep fog that shrouds the city of Dubai, signalling the start of winter.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: City Wildlife Rescue
programme.desc: Join the rescue team for a madcap chase after a red-tailed hawk. He may be unable to fly, but he sure can run! Then follow the recovery of a rare albino squirrel who can’t walk, and see how the medical team ensures he’ll be ready for the wild again.

credits.director: Stacey Hatch, David Hatch, Sharone Ostrovsky:Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Find out why islands are like mini-Earths where evolution happens before your eyes. Journey from the Galápagos to Antarctica to see how sea lions, finches, crickets, monkeys and other wildlife are finding surprising solutions to climatic problems.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Go to extremes, from the Sahara to the outback, where animals are teaching us how to survive in our heating world. Shelter in caves with chimps, see camels sip salt water, and listen as zebra finches sing to their eggs to make their chicks smaller.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Observe the eternal search for water that defines the lives of Africa’s wild animals. Witness the biggest marine feeding frenzy on Earth, see how cobras stock up on solar power, and join wildebeest herds and elephant clans on their epic migrations.

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: Enter the world of a wild dog pack whose bountiful hunting grounds are threatened when a clan of spotted hyenas moves in nearby. The vulnerable pups start to go hungry as the scavenging rivals steal their food and eventually raid their den.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producer

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: To the lion, the buffalo is not only a satisfying dinner but also dangerous. Track a herd of 300 buffalo along with the Mwamba pride, who must act quickly and carefully if they are to take down their prey.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producer

programme.title: Woodpeckers: The Hole Story
programme.desc: March to the steady drumbeat of a unique avian family. Learn why the world’s 239 woodpecker species, from massive to minute, are nature’s best lumberjacks; explore their flying, climbing, and calling skills; and get a look inside their secret nests.

credits.director: Narration: Chris Morgan Producer: Ann Johnson Prum

programme.title: Survival of the Beast S1E1 -Desert-Adapted Elephant
programme.desc: Trek across the Namibian desert with the bush elephant and learn how it keeps its cool in a place with little food or shade. Discover how this giant consumes specific plants, digs wells, and hikes endless dunes in a quest to find precious water.

credits.director: Donovan Chan, Jocelyn Little, Rob Sixsmith

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Find out why islands are like mini-Earths where evolution happens before your eyes. Journey from the Galápagos to Antarctica to see how sea lions, finches, crickets, monkeys and other wildlife are finding surprising solutions to climatic problems.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Go to extremes, from the Sahara to the outback, where animals are teaching us how to survive in our heating world. Shelter in caves with chimps, see camels sip salt water, and listen as zebra finches sing to their eggs to make their chicks smaller.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: A Wild Year on Earth S1E2 -March-April
programme.desc: Travel around the globe to witness the atmospheric shifts that take place in March, from the wave of cherry blossoms that unfold across Japan to the final raft of green sea turtles that arrive on the Great Barrier Reef islands to lay eggs.

credits.director: Karina Holden: Executive Producer, Daniella Ortega: Series Producer

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: Enter the world of a wild dog pack whose bountiful hunting grounds are threatened when a clan of spotted hyenas moves in nearby. The vulnerable pups start to go hungry as the scavenging rivals steal their food and eventually raid their den.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producer

programme.desc: To the lion, the buffalo is not only a satisfying dinner but also dangerous. Track a herd of 300 buffalo along with the Mwamba pride, who must act quickly and carefully if they are to take down their prey.

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producer

programme.desc: March to the steady drumbeat of a unique avian family. Learn why the world’s 239 woodpecker species, from massive to minute, are nature’s best lumberjacks; explore their flying, climbing, and calling skills; and get a look inside their secret nests.

credits.director: Narration: Chris Morgan Producer: Ann Johnson Prum

programme.desc: Trek across the Namibian desert with the bush elephant and learn how it keeps its cool in a place with little food or shade. Discover how this giant consumes specific plants, digs wells, and hikes endless dunes in a quest to find precious water.

credits.director: Donovan Chan, Jocelyn Little, Rob Sixsmith

programme.desc: Find out why islands are like mini-Earths where evolution happens before your eyes. Journey from the Galápagos to Antarctica to see how sea lions, finches, crickets, monkeys and other wildlife are finding surprising solutions to climatic problems.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

credits.director: CHADDEN HUNTER: Producer, BRENNAN NURTHEN: Director

credits.director: Martha Holmes, James Smith:Producer

programme.desc: March to the steady drumbeat of a unique avian family. Learn why the world’s 239 woodpecker species, from massive to minute, are nature’s best lumberjacks; explore their flying, climbing, and calling skills; and get a look inside their secret nests.

credits.director: Narration: Chris Morgan Producer: Ann Johnson Prum

programme.title: Survival of the Beast S1E1 -Desert-Adapted Elephant
programme.desc: Trek across the Namibian desert with the bush elephant and learn how it keeps its cool in a place with little food or shade. Discover how this giant consumes specific plants, digs wells, and hikes endless dunes in a quest to find precious water.

credits.director: Donovan Chan, Jocelyn Little, Rob Sixsmith

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Find out why islands are like mini-Earths where evolution happens before your eyes. Journey from the Galápagos to Antarctica to see how sea lions, finches, crickets, monkeys and other wildlife are finding surprising solutions to climatic problems.

credits.director: Shane Campbell-Stanton: Narrator; David Allen, Oliver Twitch: Producers

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: Go to extremes, from the Sahara to the outback, where animals are teaching us how to survive in our heating world. Shelter in caves with chimps, see camels sip salt water, and listen as zebra finches sing to their eggs to make their chicks smaller.
